citations.narrative:
The APHIS inspector reviewed an unexpected event report submitted on January 31, 2026 and an IACUC investigation
report dated April 17, 2026, for RBC1 and E line turkey poults on an IACUC approved protocol. On January 29, turkey
‑ ‑
poults on an IACUC approved protocol in the main poultry building were receiving supplemental electrolytes. On January
30, research staff observed increased mortality, weak poults, and birds exhibiting abnormal clinical signs including
increased vocalization, head tremors, convulsions, and dehydration. Research staff subsequently discovered
crystallization on electrolyte waterers and confirmed that poults had received electrolyte solution in bell waterers and via
oral syringe at 128 times the intended concentration because the stock solution had not been diluted prior to
administration. Animal care personnel noted that the packaging for electrolytes differed from the standard packaging and
that they had not received sufficient training to correctly dilute the product before administering it to the animals.
Excessive electrolyte administration led to severe clinical manifestations, resulting in 127 turkey poults becoming clinically
ill and mortality for an additional 141 turkey poults.
Ensure all scientists, research technicians, animal technicians, and other personnel involved in animal care, treatment,
and use are qualified for their duties, including receiving adequate oversight, standardized training, and clear procedures
so that electrolyte solutions are prepared and administered correctly. Correct from May 7, 2026, forward.

The APHIS inspector reviewed an unexpected event report submitted on January 31, 2026 and an IACUC investigation
report dated April 17, 2026, for RBC1 and E line turkey poults on an IACUC approved protocol. Heat lamps for two pens
‑ ‑
in the main poultry barn failed due to the heat lamps being connected to a single circuit that tripped the electrical breaker.
The heat lamps were required to provide zones of additional heat to support the birds.
Although the barn temperature overall remained within normal ranges, the lack of adequate heat zones resulted in about
170 turkey poults to be cold, flipped over, and weak endangering the poults’ health by causing cold stress.
Provide and maintain reliable electrical power adequate for heating, cooling, ventilation, and lighting. Correct from May 7,
2026, forward.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with Facility Representatives.
End of report.
n

narrative:
2.32(a)  Critical
   
Personnel qualifications. 
The APHIS inspector reviewed an unexpected event report submitted on January 31, 2026 and an IACUC investigation 
report dated April 17, 2026, for RBC1 and E‑line turkey poults on an IACUC‑approved protocol. On January 29, turkey 
poults on an IACUC approved protocol in the main poultry building were receiving supplemental electrolytes. On January 
30, research staff observed increased mortality, weak poults, and birds exhibiting abnormal clinical signs including 
increased vocalization, head tremors, convulsions, and dehydration. Research staff subsequently discovered 
crystallization on electrolyte waterers and confirmed that poults had received electrolyte solution in bell waterers and via 
oral syringe at 128 times the intended concentration because the stock solution had not been diluted prior to 
administration. Animal care personnel noted that the packaging for electrolytes differed from the standard packaging and 
that they had not received sufficient training to correctly dilute the product before administering it to the animals. 
 
Excessive electrolyte administration led to severe clinical manifestations, resulting in 127 turkey poults becoming clinically 
ill and mortality for an additional 141 turkey poults. 
 
Ensure all scientists, research technicians, animal technicians, and other personnel involved in animal care, treatment, 
and use are qualified for their duties, including receiving adequate oversight, standardized training, and clear procedures 
so that electrolyte solutions are prepared and administered correctly. Correct from May 7, 2026, forward. 
3.150(d)  Critical
   
Facilities, general. 
The APHIS inspector reviewed an unexpected event report submitted on January 31, 2026 and an IACUC investigation 
report dated April 17, 2026, for RBC1 and E‑line turkey poults on an IACUC‑approved protocol. Heat lamps for two pens 
in the main poultry barn failed due to the heat lamps being connected to a single circuit that tripped the electrical breaker. 
The heat lamps were required to provide zones of additional heat to support the birds. 
 
Although the barn temperature overall remained within normal ranges, the lack of adequate heat zones resulted in about 
170 turkey poults to be cold, flipped over, and weak endangering the poults’ health by causing cold stress.

Provide and maintain reliable electrical power adequate for heating, cooling, ventilation, and lighting. Correct from May 7, 
2026, forward. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with Facility Representatives. 
 
End of report. 
 
 n
